title: Amid nurse shortage, a university rolls out the welcome mat for men
excerpt: Stay up to date with our Up First newsletter sent every weekday morning.
content_html:
Stay up to date with our Up First newsletter sent every weekday morning.

Men are largely missing out on one of the hottest professions in the U.S. right now: nursing.

For registered nurses alone, the Labor Department projects there will be 189,000 job openings each year for the next decade. But only 1 in 8 nurses are men.

There have long been efforts to rewrite the script around nursing — to push back on the idea that it's women's work. Now, the economy is helping, too.

Related Story: NPR

With diminishing opportunities in male-dominated industries like manufacturing and software engineering, nursing has emerged as a stable alternative and a solid pathway to a middle-class life.

Nursing students at the University of Alabama at Birmingham are already experiencing what it's like to be in demand.

Ethan Stinson will graduate next month and go straight into a job in the psychiatric unit at the university hospital.

"I feel like it's really easy to get a psych job if you're a nurse," he says. "They're hunting people all the time."

His classmate Hatim Nasser, whose family had wanted him to study computer science, has a job lined up in the university hospital's heart and lung transplant intensive care unit.

At a time when many of their peers are unsure of what's next, the students are happy their skills are in demand.

"We're grateful for it," Nasser says.

A nursing school in the South leads the way

No doubt, stereotypes about men in nursing still abound. But slowly, they're being eroded.

At the University of Alabama at Birmingham, close to 1 in 6 nursing students are men, outpacing the national average. And nearly 1 in 5 faculty are men. In some specialties within the university hospital, male nurses are nearly as common as female nurses.

"I don't really feel like nursing isn't for men or anything like that. When I go to the hospital, I see guys all the time," says nursing student Amiri Saadiq, who's headed for a job in the bone marrow transplant intensive care unit.

For years, the nursing school has been intentional about creating a welcoming environment for men. It features male students on all its marketing materials. It sends male and female nurses into high schools to recruit students. There's an employee resource group for men in nursing at the university hospital.

Professor Curry Bordelon says all of this makes a difference.

"We're never going to flip the entire iceberg. But I can get one little ice cube at a time out of it," he says. "And when you do that, you're opening up that opportunity for change."

A different landscape in the 1990s

Things were very different three decades ago when Bordelon was getting his start in what he calls "small-town Louisiana."

Men made up only 4% of nurses back then. Bordelon first learned about nursing through his aunt, who was a nurse. He was her practice patient.

Out of high school, he worked as a volunteer firefighter and found himself drawn to the medical calls.

From there, he enrolled in a nursing program at a local community college. He remembers his first day, looking around. There were about 90 students in his first class. Just three of them were men. He recalls the instructor telling the guys that the only reason they were there was to satisfy a quota.

"That, to me, was sort of a wake-up," he says. "It was a — 'I want to find my right fit where I can strive.'"

Getting help from someone who looks like you

Bordelon found a more welcoming environment at a different nursing school. Then, he found his calling: working in a neonatal intensive care unit (NICU), where male nurses are extremely rare.

He says fathers in the NICU would sometimes get defensive when they saw him talking to their wives about breastfeeding. But he made a point to include them, too.

"'I'm here to support the entire family unit,'" he says he would tell them. "'Which means — Dad, would you like to do skin-to-skin?'"

That simple action — holding a baby to one's bare chest — is incredibly important for the baby's long-term success, Bordelon says. Not to mention, it prepares fathers for the transition home.

Bordelon is certain that having more male nurses everywhere could improve health outcomes. Studies have found that patients respond better to healthcare providers who look like them.

Nursing student Nicholas Seay, who is Black, has already experienced this during his clinical rotations. He finds older Black men often look relieved when he walks into the room. At times, they seem more at ease talking with him than with the nurse he's shadowing.

"I think they're happy to see [a] familiar face," he says.

Seay has also experienced the opposite: female patients who are not comfortable with him at their bedside. In those situations, he steps aside.

"If I was a woman, I don't know if I'd want a male student in the room either, so I really can't be mad," he says.

"Permission" to be in nursing

Even in the cocoon of the university setting, stereotypes persist.

For one, male nurses are often mistaken for doctors. Chris Jones, a graduate student pursuing a dual master's degree in nursing and business administration, says it happens when he enters patients' rooms, as well as elsewhere in the hospital.

"They would give me the resident discount in the cafeteria, and it's a good discount," he says with a laugh. "I've had to correct them."

And because nursing requires patience and compassion — traits traditionally seen as feminine — there are people who assume all male nurses are gay.

Bordelon knows persistent stereotyping gives some men pause.

"A lot of times, men need permission to be in the nursing field," he says.

He's happy that popular shows like The Pitt depict men in all kinds of healthcare roles, including nursing. And, he says, the fact that the jobs pay well doesn't hurt. The national median wage for registered nurses tops $90,000 a year.

Jones, who was the first in his family to go to college, says one reason he became a nurse was so that he'd be able to take care of himself.

"And if I wanted to start a family one day, too, that I could do that as well," he says.

In recent years, nursing has become more technologically advanced, another draw for men, says Steven Parsons, a cardiovascular operating room nurse and adjunct instructor at the University of Alabama at Birmingham.

Robots are frequently used in lung surgery and sometimes in heart surgery, he says. A robotics team that he was a part of was majority male.

"You've got guys that are kind of interested in that mechanical stuff," he says.

Still, for others, the motivation for going into nursing is personal. Connor Aderholt, a former college baseball player, says he and his brother both decided to pursue careers in nursing after observing the compassionate care their grandmother received after a stroke.

"In that vulnerable moment — in our family's vulnerable moment — it made me realize, this is what I want to do, to deal with these tough situations that people have to deal with," says Aderholt. "To be that calm in the storm for other people."

Moving the needle faster

This fall, Bordelon will assume leadership of the American Association for Men in Nursing, a national advocacy group. He knows there's still much to tackle.

"Will we ever be 100% gender neutral? I don't know that," Bordelon says. "Can we help move the needle a little faster? Sure, there's opportunities for that."

He and a colleague at Purdue University School of Nursing recently scored a big win: getting men's health officially designated as a nursing specialty. Beyond urological issues, Bordelon argues, there are many other factors that drive a shorter life expectancy for men.

"We have women's health nurse practitioners. We potentially could have men's health nurse practitioners," Bordelon says. "That's our dream. That's our goal."

sentences.zh: 在大学医院的一些专科中，男护士几乎与女护士一样常见。

blocks.html_en: "I don't really feel like nursing isn't for men or anything like that. When I go to the hospital, I see guys all the time," says nursing student Amiri Saadiq, who's headed for a job in the bone marrow transplant intensive care unit.

sentences.en: "I don't really feel like nursing isn't for men or anything like that.
sentences.zh: “我真的不觉得护理不适合男性或类似的东西。

sentences.en: When I go to the hospital, I see guys all the time," says nursing student Amiri Saadiq, who's headed for a job in the bone marrow transplant intensive care unit.
sentences.zh: 当我去医院时，我总是看到男人，”即将在骨髓移植重症监护室工作的护理专业学生阿米里·萨阿迪克 (Amiri Saadiq) 说道。

blocks.html_en: For years, the nursing school has been intentional about creating a welcoming environment for men. It features male students on all its marketing materials. It sends male and female nurses into high schools to recruit students. There's an employee resource group for men in nursing at the university hospital.

sentences.en: For years, the nursing school has been intentional about creating a welcoming environment for men.
sentences.zh: 多年来，护士学校一直致力于为男性创造一个温馨的环境。

sentences.en: It features male students on all its marketing materials.
sentences.zh: 它的所有营销材料中都以男学生为主角。

sentences.en: It sends male and female nurses into high schools to recruit students.
sentences.zh: 它派遣男女护士到高中招收学生。

sentences.en: There's an employee resource group for men in nursing at the university hospital.
sentences.zh: 大学医院有一个针对男性护理人员的员工资源小组。

blocks.html_en: Professor Curry Bordelon says all of this makes a difference.

sentences.en: Professor Curry Bordelon says all of this makes a difference.
sentences.zh: Curry Bordelon 教授表示，所有这些都会产生影响。

blocks.html_en: "We're never going to flip the entire iceberg. But I can get one little ice cube at a time out of it," he says. "And when you do that, you're opening up that opportunity for change."

sentences.en: "We're never going to flip the entire iceberg.
sentences.zh: “我们永远不会翻转整个冰山。

sentences.en: But I can get one little ice cube at a time out of it," he says.
sentences.zh: 但我一次可以从中取出一小块冰块，”他说。

sentences.en: "And when you do that, you're opening up that opportunity for change."
sentences.zh: “当你这样做时，你就开启了变革的机会。”

blocks.html_en: A different landscape in the 1990s

sentences.en: A different landscape in the 1990s
sentences.zh: 20世纪90年代的另一番景象

blocks.html_en: Things were very different three decades ago when Bordelon was getting his start in what he calls "small-town Louisiana."

sentences.en: Things were very different three decades ago when Bordelon was getting his start in what he calls "small-town Louisiana."
sentences.zh: 三十年前，当博德隆在他所谓的“路易斯安那州小镇”开始创业时，情况已大不相同。

blocks.html_en: Men made up only 4% of nurses back then. Bordelon first learned about nursing through his aunt, who was a nurse. He was her practice patient.

sentences.en: Men made up only 4% of nurses back then.
sentences.zh: 当时男性护士只占4%。

sentences.en: Bordelon first learned about nursing through his aunt, who was a nurse.
sentences.zh: 博德隆第一次学习护理是通过他的护士阿姨。

sentences.en: He was her practice patient.
sentences.zh: 他是她的实习病人。

blocks.html_en: Out of high school, he worked as a volunteer firefighter and found himself drawn to the medical calls.

sentences.en: Out of high school, he worked as a volunteer firefighter and found himself drawn to the medical calls.
sentences.zh: 高中毕业后，他成为一名志愿消防员，并发现自己被医疗电话所吸引。

blocks.html_en: From there, he enrolled in a nursing program at a local community college. He remembers his first day, looking around. There were about 90 students in his first class. Just three of them were men. He recalls the instructor telling the guys that the only reason they were there was to satisfy a quota.

sentences.en: From there, he enrolled in a nursing program at a local community college.
sentences.zh: 从那里，他报名参加了当地社区大学的护理课程。

sentences.en: He remembers his first day, looking around.
sentences.zh: 他记得第一天，环顾四周。

sentences.en: There were about 90 students in his first class.
sentences.zh: 他的第一堂课大约有90名学生。

sentences.en: Just three of them were men.
sentences.zh: 其中只有三个是男性。

sentences.en: He recalls the instructor telling the guys that the only reason they were there was to satisfy a quota.
sentences.zh: 他记得教练告诉队员们，他们来这里的唯一原因是为了完成配额。

blocks.html_en: "That, to me, was sort of a wake-up," he says. "It was a — 'I want to find my right fit where I can strive.'"

sentences.en: "That, to me, was sort of a wake-up," he says.
sentences.zh: “对我来说，这有点像警醒，”他说。

sentences.en: "It was a — 'I want to find my right fit where I can strive.'"
sentences.zh: “那是——‘我想找到适合我的地方，我可以努力奋斗。’”

blocks.html_en: Getting help from someone who looks like you

sentences.en: Getting help from someone who looks like you
sentences.zh: 向与您相似的人寻求帮助

blocks.html_en: Bordelon found a more welcoming environment at a different nursing school. Then, he found his calling: working in a neonatal intensive care unit (NICU), where male nurses are extremely rare.

sentences.en: Bordelon found a more welcoming environment at a different nursing school.
sentences.zh: 博德隆在另一所护士学校找到了更受欢迎的环境。

sentences.en: Then, he found his calling: working in a neonatal intensive care unit (NICU), where male nurses are extremely rare.
sentences.zh: 然后，他找到了自己的使命：在新生儿重症监护室（NICU）工作，那里的男护士极其稀少。

blocks.html_en: He says fathers in the NICU would sometimes get defensive when they saw him talking to their wives about breastfeeding. But he made a point to include them, too.

sentences.en: He says fathers in the NICU would sometimes get defensive when they saw him talking to their wives about breastfeeding.
sentences.zh: 他说，新生儿重症监护室的父亲们有时会因为看到他与妻子谈论母乳喂养而产生戒心。

sentences.en: But he made a point to include them, too.
sentences.zh: 但他也强调将它们也包括在内。

blocks.html_en: "'I'm here to support the entire family unit,'" he says he would tell them. "'Which means — Dad, would you like to do skin-to-skin?'"

sentences.en: "'I'm here to support the entire family unit,'" he says he would tell them.
sentences.zh: “‘我来这里是为了支持整个家庭，’”他说他会告诉他们。

sentences.en: "'Which means — Dad, would you like to do skin-to-skin?'"
sentences.zh: “‘这意味着——爸爸，你愿意肌肤相亲吗？’”

blocks.html_en: That simple action — holding a baby to one's bare chest — is incredibly important for the baby's long-term success, Bordelon says. Not to mention, it prepares fathers for the transition home.

sentences.en: That simple action — holding a baby to one's bare chest — is incredibly important for the baby's long-term success, Bordelon says.
sentences.zh: 博德隆说，这个简单的动作——将婴儿抱在裸露的胸前——对于婴儿的长期成功非常重要。

sentences.en: Not to mention, it prepares fathers for the transition home.
sentences.zh: 更不用说，它为父亲们为过渡家庭做好准备。

blocks.html_en: Bordelon is certain that having more male nurses everywhere could improve health outcomes. Studies have found that patients respond better to healthcare providers who look like them.

sentences.en: Bordelon is certain that having more male nurses everywhere could improve health outcomes.
sentences.zh: 博德隆确信，各地拥有更多的男护士可以改善健康状况。

sentences.en: Studies have found that patients respond better to healthcare providers who look like them.
sentences.zh: 研究发现，患者对与自己相似的医疗服务提供者的反应更好。

blocks.html_en: Nursing student Nicholas Seay, who is Black, has already experienced this during his clinical rotations. He finds older Black men often look relieved when he walks into the room. At times, they seem more at ease talking with him than with the nurse he's shadowing.

sentences.en: Nursing student Nicholas Seay, who is Black, has already experienced this during his clinical rotations.
sentences.zh: 黑人护理学生尼古拉斯·西伊（Nicholas Seay）在临床轮转期间已经经历过这种情况。

sentences.en: He finds older Black men often look relieved when he walks into the room.
sentences.zh: 他发现当他走进房间时，年长的黑人往往看起来如释重负。

sentences.en: At times, they seem more at ease talking with him than with the nurse he's shadowing.
sentences.zh: 有时，他们与他交谈似乎比与他跟随的护士交谈更自在。

blocks.html_en: "I think they're happy to see [a] familiar face," he says.

sentences.en: "I think they're happy to see [a] familiar face," he says.
sentences.zh: “我认为他们很高兴看到熟悉的面孔，”他说。

blocks.html_en: Seay has also experienced the opposite: female patients who are not comfortable with him at their bedside. In those situations, he steps aside.

sentences.en: Seay has also experienced the opposite: female patients who are not comfortable with him at their bedside.
sentences.zh: 西伊也经历过相反的情况：女性患者对他在床边感到不舒服。

sentences.en: In those situations, he steps aside.
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，他会退到一边。

blocks.html_en: "If I was a woman, I don't know if I'd want a male student in the room either, so I really can't be mad," he says.

sentences.en: "If I was a woman, I don't know if I'd want a male student in the room either, so I really can't be mad," he says.
sentences.zh: “如果我是一名女性，我也不知道我是否希望房间里有一名男学生，所以我真的不能生气，”他说。

blocks.html_en: "Permission" to be in nursing

sentences.en: "Permission" to be in nursing
sentences.zh: “许可”从事护理工作

blocks.html_en: Even in the cocoon of the university setting, stereotypes persist.

sentences.en: Even in the cocoon of the university setting, stereotypes persist.
sentences.zh: 即使在大学环境的茧中，刻板印象仍然存在。

blocks.html_en: For one, male nurses are often mistaken for doctors. Chris Jones, a graduate student pursuing a dual master's degree in nursing and business administration, says it happens when he enters patients' rooms, as well as elsewhere in the hospital.

sentences.en: For one, male nurses are often mistaken for doctors.
sentences.zh: 其一，男护士经常被误认为是医生。

sentences.en: Chris Jones, a graduate student pursuing a dual master's degree in nursing and business administration, says it happens when he enters patients' rooms, as well as elsewhere in the hospital.
sentences.zh: 正在攻读护理和工商管理双硕士学位的研究生克里斯·琼斯 (Chris Jones) 表示，当他进入病房以及医院的其他地方时，就会发生这种情况。

blocks.html_en: "They would give me the resident discount in the cafeteria, and it's a good discount," he says with a laugh. "I've had to correct them."

sentences.en: "They would give me the resident discount in the cafeteria, and it's a good discount," he says with a laugh.
sentences.zh: “他们会给我食堂的居民折扣，这是一个很好的折扣，”他笑着说。

sentences.en: "I've had to correct them."
sentences.zh: “我必须纠正它们。”

blocks.html_en: And because nursing requires patience and compassion — traits traditionally seen as feminine — there are people who assume all male nurses are gay.

sentences.en: And because nursing requires patience and compassion — traits traditionally seen as feminine — there are people who assume all male nurses are gay.
sentences.zh: 由于护理需要耐心和同情心——这些特质传统上被视为女性特质——所以有人认为所有男护士都是同性恋。

blocks.html_en: Bordelon knows persistent stereotyping gives some men pause.

sentences.en: Bordelon knows persistent stereotyping gives some men pause.
sentences.zh: 博德隆知道，持续存在的刻板印象会让一些男人犹豫不决。

blocks.html_en: "A lot of times, men need permission to be in the nursing field," he says.

sentences.en: "A lot of times, men need permission to be in the nursing field," he says.
sentences.zh: “很多时候，男性需要获得许可才能进入护理领域，”他说。

blocks.html_en: He's happy that popular shows like The Pitt depict men in all kinds of healthcare roles, including nursing. And, he says, the fact that the jobs pay well doesn't hurt. The national median wage for registered nurses tops $90,000 a year.

sentences.en: He's happy that popular shows like The Pitt depict men in all kinds of healthcare roles, including nursing.
sentences.zh: 他很高兴像《皮特》这样的热门节目描绘了男性扮演各种医疗保健角色，包括护理。

sentences.en: And, he says, the fact that the jobs pay well doesn't hurt.
sentences.zh: 而且，他说，工作报酬丰厚这一事实也没有坏处。

sentences.en: The national median wage for registered nurses tops $90,000 a year.
sentences.zh: 全国注册护士的平均工资每年高达 90,000 美元。

blocks.html_en: Jones, who was the first in his family to go to college, says one reason he became a nurse was so that he'd be able to take care of himself.

sentences.en: Jones, who was the first in his family to go to college, says one reason he became a nurse was so that he'd be able to take care of himself.
sentences.zh: 琼斯是家里第一个上大学的人，他说他成为一名护士的原因之一是为了能够照顾自己。

blocks.html_en: "And if I wanted to start a family one day, too, that I could do that as well," he says.

sentences.en: "And if I wanted to start a family one day, too, that I could do that as well," he says.
sentences.zh: “如果有一天我也想组建一个家庭，我也可以这么做，”他说。

blocks.html_en: In recent years, nursing has become more technologically advanced, another draw for men, says Steven Parsons, a cardiovascular operating room nurse and adjunct instructor at the University of Alabama at Birmingham.

sentences.en: In recent years, nursing has become more technologically advanced, another draw for men, says Steven Parsons, a cardiovascular operating room nurse and adjunct instructor at the University of Alabama at Birmingham.
sentences.zh: 阿拉巴马大学伯明翰分校心血管手术室护士兼兼职讲师史蒂文·帕森斯（Steven Parsons）表示，近年来，护理技术变得更加先进，这是对男性的另一个吸引力。

blocks.html_en: Robots are frequently used in lung surgery and sometimes in heart surgery, he says. A robotics team that he was a part of was majority male.

sentences.en: Robots are frequently used in lung surgery and sometimes in heart surgery, he says.
sentences.zh: 他说，机器人经常用于肺部手术，有时也用于心脏手术。

sentences.en: A robotics team that he was a part of was majority male.
sentences.zh: 他所在的一个机器人团队以男性为主。

blocks.html_en: "You've got guys that are kind of interested in that mechanical stuff," he says.

sentences.en: "You've got guys that are kind of interested in that mechanical stuff," he says.
sentences.zh: “有些人对机械东西有点感兴趣，”他说。

blocks.html_en: Still, for others, the motivation for going into nursing is personal. Connor Aderholt, a former college baseball player, says he and his brother both decided to pursue careers in nursing after observing the compassionate care their grandmother received after a stroke.

sentences.en: Still, for others, the motivation for going into nursing is personal.
sentences.zh: 不过，对于其他人来说，进入护理行业的动机是个人的。

sentences.en: Connor Aderholt, a former college baseball player, says he and his brother both decided to pursue careers in nursing after observing the compassionate care their grandmother received after a stroke.
sentences.zh: 前大学棒球运动员康纳·阿德霍特 (Connor Aderholt) 表示，在看到祖母中风后得到的富有同情心的护理后，他和弟弟都决定从事护理职业。

blocks.html_en: "In that vulnerable moment — in our family's vulnerable moment — it made me realize, this is what I want to do, to deal with these tough situations that people have to deal with," says Aderholt. "To be that calm in the storm for other people."

sentences.en: "In that vulnerable moment — in our family's vulnerable moment — it made me realize, this is what I want to do, to deal with these tough situations that people have to deal with," says Aderholt.
sentences.zh: “在那个脆弱的时刻——在我们家庭的脆弱时刻——它让我意识到，这就是我想做的事情，来应对人们必须应对的这些艰难处境，”阿德霍特说。

sentences.en: "To be that calm in the storm for other people."
sentences.zh: “为了其他人，在暴风雨中保持冷静。”

blocks.html_en: Moving the needle faster

sentences.en: Moving the needle faster
sentences.zh: 更快地移动针

blocks.html_en: This fall, Bordelon will assume leadership of the American Association for Men in Nursing, a national advocacy group. He knows there's still much to tackle.

sentences.en: This fall, Bordelon will assume leadership of the American Association for Men in Nursing, a national advocacy group.
sentences.zh: 今年秋天，博德隆将担任全国倡导组织美国男性护理协会的领导职务。

sentences.en: He knows there's still much to tackle.
sentences.zh: 他知道还有很多事情需要解决。

blocks.html_en: "Will we ever be 100% gender neutral? I don't know that," Bordelon says. "Can we help move the needle a little faster? Sure, there's opportunities for that."

sentences.en: "Will we ever be 100% gender neutral?
sentences.zh: “我们会达到 100% 性别中立吗？

sentences.en: I don't know that," Bordelon says.
sentences.zh: 我不知道，”博德隆说。

sentences.en: "Can we help move the needle a little faster?
sentences.zh: “我们能帮助进展得更快一点吗？

sentences.en: Sure, there's opportunities for that."
sentences.zh: 当然，这是有机会的。”

blocks.html_en: He and a colleague at Purdue University School of Nursing recently scored a big win: getting men's health officially designated as a nursing specialty. Beyond urological issues, Bordelon argues, there are many other factors that drive a shorter life expectancy for men.

sentences.en: He and a colleague at Purdue University School of Nursing recently scored a big win: getting men's health officially designated as a nursing specialty.
sentences.zh: 他和普渡大学护理学院的一位同事最近取得了重大胜利：将男性健康正式指定为护理专业。

sentences.en: Beyond urological issues, Bordelon argues, there are many other factors that drive a shorter life expectancy for men.
sentences.zh: 博德隆认为，除了泌尿系统问题之外，还有许多其他因素导致男性预期寿命缩短。

blocks.html_en: "We have women's health nurse practitioners. We potentially could have men's health nurse practitioners," Bordelon says. "That's our dream. That's our goal."

sentences.en: "We have women's health nurse practitioners.
sentences.zh: “我们有女性保健执业护士。

sentences.en: We potentially could have men's health nurse practitioners," Bordelon says.
sentences.zh: 我们可能会有男性健康护士从业人员，”博德隆说。

sentences.en: "That's our dream.
sentences.zh: “那是我们的梦想。

sentences.en: That's our goal."
sentences.zh: 这就是我们的目标。”

sentences.en: Transcript

blocks.html_en: AILSA CHANG, HOST:

sentences.en: AILSA CHANG, HOST:
sentences.zh: 张艾莎，主持人：

blocks.html_en: Nursing is the largest healthcare profession in the U.S., and it is growing. But only about 1 in 8 nurses are men. Still, that is double what it was a couple decades ago thanks to efforts to draw in more men. NPR's Andrea Hsu reports on how one school in the South is leading the way.

sentences.en: Nursing is the largest healthcare profession in the U.S., and it is growing.
sentences.zh: 护理是美国最大的医疗保健行业，而且还在不断增长。

sentences.en: But only about 1 in 8 nurses are men.
sentences.zh: 但只有大约八分之一的护士是男性。

sentences.en: Still, that is double what it was a couple decades ago thanks to efforts to draw in more men.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，由于吸引更多男性的努力，这一数字仍然是几十年前的两倍。

sentences.en: NPR's Andrea Hsu reports on how one school in the South is leading the way.
sentences.zh: NPR 的 Andrea Hsu 报道了南方一所学校如何处于领先地位。

blocks.html_en: ANDREA HSU, BYLINE: The simulation suite inside the University of Alabama at Birmingham School of Nursing looks just like a hospital. At a sink outside a mock operating room, adjunct instructor Steven Parsons shows students how to do what's called a long scrub, thoroughly washing their hands and arms before entering open heart surgery.

sentences.en: ANDREA HSU, BYLINE: The simulation suite inside the University of Alabama at Birmingham School of Nursing looks just like a hospital.
sentences.zh: ANDREA HSU 连线：阿拉巴马大学伯明翰护理学院内的模拟套件看起来就像一家医院。

sentences.en: At a sink outside a mock operating room, adjunct instructor Steven Parsons shows students how to do what's called a long scrub, thoroughly washing their hands and arms before entering open heart surgery.
sentences.zh: 在模拟手术室外的水槽里，兼职教练史蒂文·帕森斯向学生们展示如何进行所谓的长时间擦洗，在进入心脏直视手术之前彻底清洗双手和手臂。

blocks.html_en: STEVEN PARSONS: 'Cause I want to see your arm go up more. Up, up, up, up, up, up, up, up. There we go.

sentences.en: STEVEN PARSONS: 'Cause I want to see your arm go up more.
sentences.zh: 史蒂文·帕森斯：因为我想看到你的手臂举得更多。

sentences.en: Up, up, up, up, up, up, up, up.
sentences.zh: 上，上，上，上，上，上，上，上。

sentences.en: There we go.
sentences.zh: 我们开始吧。

blocks.html_en: HSU: A little later, the class practices logrolling a patient off a stretcher and onto a bed.

sentences.en: HSU: A little later, the class practices logrolling a patient off a stretcher and onto a bed.
sentences.zh: HSU：过了一会儿，全班练习用滚轮将病人从担架上抬到床上。

blocks.html_en: PARSONS: OK.

sentences.en: PARSONS: OK.
sentences.zh: 帕森斯：好的。

blocks.html_en: UNIDENTIFIED STUDENT #1: I got...

sentences.en: UNIDENTIFIED STUDENT #1: I got...
sentences.zh: 身份不明的学生#1：我……

blocks.html_en: PARSONS: And you're going to count down to three 'cause you're stabilizing everything.

sentences.en: PARSONS: And you're going to count down to three 'cause you're stabilizing everything.
sentences.zh: 帕森斯：你要倒数三，因为你正在稳定一切。

blocks.html_en: UNIDENTIFIED STUDENT #2: OK.

sentences.en: UNIDENTIFIED STUDENT #2: OK.
sentences.zh: 身份不明的学生#2：好的。

sentences.en: UNIDENTIFIED STUDENT #2: One, two, three.
sentences.zh: 身份不明的学生#2：一、二、三。

blocks.html_en: HSU: There are a dozen students taking this elective focused on operating room skills. Close to half of them are men. Certain specialties draw more men. This is one of them. But the University of Alabama at Birmingham does have more male nursing students than the national average. Amiri Saadiq, who just logrolled the mannequin, says as a student here, he hasn't felt alone.

sentences.en: HSU: There are a dozen students taking this elective focused on operating room skills.
sentences.zh: HSU：有十几名学生选修这门手术室技能的选修课。

sentences.en: Close to half of them are men.
sentences.zh: 其中接近一半是男性。

sentences.en: Certain specialties draw more men.
sentences.zh: 某些专业吸引更多男性。

sentences.en: This is one of them.
sentences.zh: 这是其中之一。

sentences.en: But the University of Alabama at Birmingham does have more male nursing students than the national average.
sentences.zh: 但阿拉巴马大学伯明翰分校的男护理生人数确实多于全国平均水平。

sentences.en: Amiri Saadiq, who just logrolled the mannequin, says as a student here, he hasn't felt alone.
sentences.zh: 阿米里·萨阿迪克 (Amiri Saadiq) 刚刚与模特进行了对峙，他说，作为这里的学生，他并不感到孤独。

blocks.html_en: AMIRI SAADIQ: I don't really feel like nursing isn't for men or anything like that. I mean, when I go to the hospital, I see guys all the time.

sentences.en: AMIRI SAADIQ: I don't really feel like nursing isn't for men or anything like that.
sentences.zh: AMIRI SAADIQ：我真的不觉得护理不适合男性或类似的东西。

sentences.en: I mean, when I go to the hospital, I see guys all the time.
sentences.zh: 我的意思是，当我去医院时，我总是看到男人。

blocks.html_en: HSU: That culture is something the university has worked on for years. The nursing school features male students on all its marketing materials. They send male and female nurses into high schools to recruit students. Nearly 1 in 5 faculty members are men. Professor Curry Bordelon says all that makes a difference.

sentences.en: HSU: That culture is something the university has worked on for years.
sentences.zh: HSU：这种文化是大学多年来致力于发展的东西。

sentences.en: The nursing school features male students on all its marketing materials.
sentences.zh: 这所护士学校的所有营销材料中都以男学生为特色。

sentences.en: They send male and female nurses into high schools to recruit students.
sentences.zh: 他们派男女护士到高中招收学生。

sentences.en: Nearly 1 in 5 faculty members are men.
sentences.zh: 近五分之一的教职人员是男性。

sentences.en: Professor Curry Bordelon says all that makes a difference.
sentences.zh: Curry Bordelon 教授表示，一切都会产生影响。

blocks.html_en: CURRY BORDELON: Students feel accepted. They feel like they belong.

sentences.en: CURRY BORDELON: Students feel accepted.
sentences.zh: CURRY BORDELON：学生感到被接受。

sentences.en: They feel like they belong.
sentences.zh: 他们感觉自己属于这里。

blocks.html_en: HSU: Three decades ago, back home in Louisiana, Bordelon experienced something different. He first learned about nursing through his aunt, who was a nurse.

sentences.en: HSU: Three decades ago, back home in Louisiana, Bordelon experienced something different.
sentences.zh: HSU：三十年前，回到路易斯安那州的家乡，博德隆经历了一些不同的事情。

sentences.en: He first learned about nursing through his aunt, who was a nurse.
sentences.zh: 他第一次学习护理是通过他的护士阿姨。

blocks.html_en: BORDELON: I was her practice patient, if you will.

sentences.en: BORDELON: I was her practice patient, if you will.
sentences.zh: 博德隆：如果你愿意的话，我是她的实习病人。

blocks.html_en: HSU: Out of high school, he worked as a volunteer fireman.

sentences.en: HSU: Out of high school, he worked as a volunteer fireman.
sentences.zh: HSU：高中毕业后，他成为一名志愿消防员。

blocks.html_en: BORDELON: I really enjoyed going on the medical calls.

sentences.en: BORDELON: I really enjoyed going on the medical calls.
sentences.zh: BORDELON：我真的很喜欢接听医疗电话。

blocks.html_en: HSU: And from there, he decided to enroll in a nursing program at a community college. He remembers his first day looking around.

sentences.en: HSU: And from there, he decided to enroll in a nursing program at a community college.
sentences.zh: HSU：从那时起，他决定在一所社区大学参加护理课程。

sentences.en: He remembers his first day looking around.
sentences.zh: 他记得第一天环顾四周。

blocks.html_en: BORDELON: There were about 90 people in that first class, that first nursing class that you took. There were only three male students.

sentences.en: BORDELON: There were about 90 people in that first class, that first nursing class that you took.
sentences.zh: BORDELON：你参加的第一堂护理课，也就是你参加的第一堂护理课，大约有 90 人。

sentences.en: There were only three male students.
sentences.zh: 男学生只有三个。

blocks.html_en: HSU: He recalls the instructor telling the guys that the only reason they were there was to satisfy a quota.

sentences.en: HSU: He recalls the instructor telling the guys that the only reason they were there was to satisfy a quota.
sentences.zh: HSU：他记得教练告诉队员们，他们来这里的唯一原因是为了完成配额。

blocks.html_en: BORDELON: That, to me, was sort of a wake-up. It was a, I want to find my right fit where I can strive.

sentences.en: BORDELON: That, to me, was sort of a wake-up.
sentences.zh: BORDELON：对我来说，这算是一种警醒。

sentences.en: It was a, I want to find my right fit where I can strive.
sentences.zh: 这是一个，我想找到适合我的地方，我可以努力奋斗。

blocks.html_en: HSU: Bordelon found a different nursing school and found his calling, working in neonatal intensive care, where male nurses are extremely rare. He says fathers in the NICU would sometimes get defensive when they saw him talking to their wives about breastfeeding. But he made a point to tell them...

sentences.en: HSU: Bordelon found a different nursing school and found his calling, working in neonatal intensive care, where male nurses are extremely rare.
sentences.zh: HSU：博德隆找到了一所不同的护士学校，并找到了自己的使命，在新生儿重症监护室工作，那里的男护士极其罕见。

sentences.en: He says fathers in the NICU would sometimes get defensive when they saw him talking to their wives about breastfeeding.
sentences.zh: 他说，新生儿重症监护室的父亲们有时会因为看到他与妻子谈论母乳喂养而产生戒心。

sentences.en: But he made a point to tell them...
sentences.zh: 但他特意告诉他们……

blocks.html_en: BORDELON: I'm here to support the entire family unit, which means, Dad, would you like to do skin-to-skin?

sentences.en: BORDELON: I'm here to support the entire family unit, which means, Dad, would you like to do skin-to-skin?
sentences.zh: BORDELON：我来这里是为了支持整个家庭，这意味着，爸爸，你愿意进行肌肤接触吗？

blocks.html_en: HSU: Which he says is incredibly important for the baby's long-term success.

sentences.en: HSU: Which he says is incredibly important for the baby's long-term success.
sentences.zh: HSU：他说这对于宝宝的长期成功非常重要。

blocks.html_en: BORDELON: So I've had a lot of positive feedback from the fathers in the neonatal intensive care who embraced the inclusiveness. Not to mention, it prepares them more for the post-transition home.

sentences.en: BORDELON: So I've had a lot of positive feedback from the fathers in the neonatal intensive care who embraced the inclusiveness.
sentences.zh: BORDELON：所以我从新生儿重症监护室的父亲们那里得到了很多积极的反馈，他们支持包容性。

sentences.en: Not to mention, it prepares them more for the post-transition home.
sentences.zh: 更不用说，这让他们为过渡后的家做好更多准备。

blocks.html_en: HSU: Bordelon says having more male nurses everywhere could improve outcomes. Studies have found patients respond better to healthcare workers who look like them. Nursing student Nicholas Seay has already experienced this during clinical rotations. As a young Black man, he finds older Black men sometimes look relieved when he enters the room, and they open up to him.

sentences.en: HSU: Bordelon says having more male nurses everywhere could improve outcomes.
sentences.zh: HSU：博德隆说，各地拥有更多的男护士可以改善结果。

sentences.en: Studies have found patients respond better to healthcare workers who look like them.
sentences.zh: 研究发现，患者对与自己相似的医护人员反应更好。

sentences.en: Nursing student Nicholas Seay has already experienced this during clinical rotations.
sentences.zh: 护理专业学生尼古拉斯·西伊（Nicholas Seay）在临床轮转期间已经经历过这种情况。

sentences.en: As a young Black man, he finds older Black men sometimes look relieved when he enters the room, and they open up to him.
sentences.zh: 作为一名年轻的黑人，他发现当他进入房间时，年长的黑人有时会显得如释重负，并向他敞开心扉。

blocks.html_en: NICHOLAS SEAY: I had this one patient in the ICU, like, most of the time, they're, like, really sick.

sentences.en: NICHOLAS SEAY: I had this one patient in the ICU, like, most of the time, they're, like, really sick.
sentences.zh: 尼古拉斯·西：我在重症监护室里收治了一位病人，大多数时候，他们病得很厉害。

blocks.html_en: HSU: And not able to talk, but not this guy.

sentences.en: HSU: And not able to talk, but not this guy.
sentences.zh: HSU：不能说话，但这个人不行。

blocks.html_en: SEAY: I found myself having to be, like, OK. I got to, like - I got to leave 'cause we're just in here yapping. I've already given your medication, sir. Like, I'll come back.

sentences.en: SEAY: I found myself having to be, like, OK.
sentences.zh: SEAY：我发现自己必须要，好吧。

sentences.en: I got to, like - I got to leave 'cause we're just in here yapping.
sentences.zh: 我必须，就像——我必须离开，因为我们只是在这里大喊大叫。

sentences.en: I've already given your medication, sir.
sentences.zh: 我已经给你开药了，先生。

sentences.en: Like, I'll come back.
sentences.zh: 就像，我会回来的。

blocks.html_en: HSU: Now, Seay has also experienced female patients who are not comfortable with him at their bedside. In those situations, he steps aside.

sentences.en: HSU: Now, Seay has also experienced female patients who are not comfortable with him at their bedside.
sentences.zh: HSU：现在，Seay 也遇到过一些女性患者对他在床边感到不舒服的情况。

sentences.en: In those situations, he steps aside.
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，他会退到一边。

blocks.html_en: SEAY: If I was a woman, I don't know if I would want a male student in the room either. So I really can't be mad.

sentences.en: SEAY: If I was a woman, I don't know if I would want a male student in the room either.
sentences.zh: SEAY：如果我是一名女性，我也不知道我是否会想要一个男学生在房间里。

sentences.en: So I really can't be mad.
sentences.zh: 所以我真的不能生气。

blocks.html_en: HSU: Even in this university setting, stereotypes do persist. Male nurses are often mistaken for doctors, for one. And then there are people who assume all male nurses are gay. Curry Bordelon knows those kinds of stereotypes give some men pause.

sentences.en: HSU: Even in this university setting, stereotypes do persist.
sentences.zh: HSU：即使在大学环境中，刻板印象也仍然存在。

sentences.en: Male nurses are often mistaken for doctors, for one.
sentences.zh: 男护士经常被误认为是医生。

sentences.en: And then there are people who assume all male nurses are gay.
sentences.zh: 还有人认为所有男护士都是同性恋。

sentences.en: Curry Bordelon knows those kinds of stereotypes give some men pause.
sentences.zh: 库里·博德隆知道这种刻板印象会让一些男人犹豫不决。

blocks.html_en: BORDELON: A lot of times, men need permission to be in the nursing field.

sentences.en: BORDELON: A lot of times, men need permission to be in the nursing field.
sentences.zh: BORDELON：很多时候，男性需要获得许可才能进入护理领域。

blocks.html_en: HSU: He's happy that popular shows like "The Pitt" depict men in all kinds of healthcare roles, including nursing. And he says the fact that the jobs pay well doesn't hurt.

sentences.en: HSU: He's happy that popular shows like "The Pitt" depict men in all kinds of healthcare roles, including nursing.
sentences.zh: HSU：他很高兴像《皮特》这样的热门节目描绘了男性扮演各种医疗保健角色，包括护理。

sentences.en: And he says the fact that the jobs pay well doesn't hurt.
sentences.zh: 他还表示，工作报酬丰厚这一事实并没有什么坏处。

blocks.html_en: BORDELON: Will we ever be 100% gender neutral? I don't know that. Can we help move the needle a little faster? Sure. There's opportunities for that.

sentences.en: BORDELON: Will we ever be 100% gender neutral?
sentences.zh: BORDELON：我们会实现 100% 性别中立吗？

sentences.en: I don't know that.
sentences.zh: 我不知道。

sentences.en: Can we help move the needle a little faster?
sentences.zh: 我们能帮助进展得更快一点吗？

sentences.en: Sure.
sentences.zh: 当然。

sentences.en: There's opportunities for that.
sentences.zh: 有这样的机会。

blocks.html_en: HSU: Bordelon and a colleague at Purdue recently scored a big win, getting men's health officially designated as a nursing specialty. He's already imagining what a difference that will make.

sentences.en: HSU: Bordelon and a colleague at Purdue recently scored a big win, getting men's health officially designated as a nursing specialty.
sentences.zh: HSU：博德隆和普渡大学的一位同事最近取得了重大胜利，将男性健康正式指定为护理专业。

sentences.en: He's already imagining what a difference that will make.
sentences.zh: 他已经在想象这将会带来多大的改变。

blocks.html_en: Andrea Hsu, NPR News, Birmingham, Alabama.

sentences.en: Andrea Hsu, NPR News, Birmingham, Alabama.

content_html_zh:
请随时关注我们每个工作日早上发送的 Up First 时事通讯。

男性很大程度上错过了目前美国最热门的职业之一：护理。

仅就注册护士而言，劳工部预计未来十年每年将有 189,000 个职位空缺。 但只有八分之一的护士是男性。

长期以来，人们一直在努力围绕护理重写剧本，以推翻护理是女性工作的观点。 现在，经济也在发挥作用。

相关报道：NPR

随着制造业和软件工程等男性主导行业的机会减少，护理已成为一种稳定的替代选择和通往中产阶级生活的坚实途径。

阿拉巴马大学伯明翰分校的护理学生已经体验到了需求的滋味。

伊森·斯汀森将于下个月毕业，并直接在大学医院的精神科工作。

“我觉得如果你是一名护士，找到一份心理工作真的很容易，”他说。 “他们一直在猎杀人类。”

他的同学哈蒂姆·纳赛尔（Hatim Nasser）的家人希望他学习计算机科学，他在大学医院的心肺移植重症监护室找到了一份工作。

当许多同龄人不确定接下来会发生什么时，学生们很高兴他们的技能受到需求。

“我们对此表示感谢，”纳赛尔说。

南方的一所护士学校引领潮流

毫无疑问，人们对男性护理人员的刻板印象仍然存在。 但慢慢地，它们正在被侵蚀。

在阿拉巴马大学伯明翰分校，近六分之一的护理学生是男性，超过了全国平均水平。 近五分之一的教师是男性。 在大学医院的一些专科中，男护士几乎与女护士一样常见。

“我真的不觉得护理不适合男性或类似的东西。 当我去医院时，我总是看到男人，”即将在骨髓移植重症监护室工作的护理专业学生阿米里·萨阿迪克 (Amiri Saadiq) 说道。

多年来，护士学校一直致力于为男性创造一个温馨的环境。 它的所有营销材料中都以男学生为主角。 它派遣男女护士到高中招收学生。 大学医院有一个针对男性护理人员的员工资源小组。

Curry Bordelon 教授表示，所有这些都会产生影响。

“我们永远不会翻转整个冰山。 但我一次可以从中取出一小块冰块，”他说。 “当你这样做时，你就开启了变革的机会。”

20世纪90年代的另一番景象

三十年前，当博德隆在他所谓的“路易斯安那州小镇”开始创业时，情况已大不相同。

当时男性护士只占4%。 博德隆第一次学习护理是通过他的护士阿姨。 他是她的实习病人。

高中毕业后，他成为一名志愿消防员，并发现自己被医疗电话所吸引。

从那里，他报名参加了当地社区大学的护理课程。 他记得第一天，环顾四周。 他的第一堂课大约有90名学生。 其中只有三个是男性。 他记得教练告诉队员们，他们来这里的唯一原因是为了完成配额。

“对我来说，这有点像警醒，”他说。 “那是——‘我想找到适合我的地方，我可以努力奋斗。’”

向与您相似的人寻求帮助

博德隆在另一所护士学校找到了更受欢迎的环境。 然后，他找到了自己的使命：在新生儿重症监护室（NICU）工作，那里的男护士极其稀少。

他说，新生儿重症监护室的父亲们有时会因为看到他与妻子谈论母乳喂养而产生戒心。 但他也强调将它们也包括在内。

“‘我来这里是为了支持整个家庭，’”他说他会告诉他们。 “‘这意味着——爸爸，你愿意肌肤相亲吗？’”

博德隆说，这个简单的动作——将婴儿抱在裸露的胸前——对于婴儿的长期成功非常重要。 更不用说，它为父亲们为过渡家庭做好准备。

博德隆确信，各地拥有更多的男护士可以改善健康状况。 研究发现，患者对与自己相似的医疗服务提供者的反应更好。

黑人护理学生尼古拉斯·西伊（Nicholas Seay）在临床轮转期间已经经历过这种情况。 他发现当他走进房间时，年长的黑人往往看起来如释重负。 有时，他们与他交谈似乎比与他跟随的护士交谈更自在。

“我认为他们很高兴看到熟悉的面孔，”他说。

西伊也经历过相反的情况：女性患者对他在床边感到不舒服。 在这种情况下，他会退到一边。

“如果我是一名女性，我也不知道我是否希望房间里有一名男学生，所以我真的不能生气，”他说。

“许可”从事护理工作

即使在大学环境的茧中，刻板印象仍然存在。

其一，男护士经常被误认为是医生。 正在攻读护理和工商管理双硕士学位的研究生克里斯·琼斯 (Chris Jones) 表示，当他进入病房以及医院的其他地方时，就会发生这种情况。

“他们会给我食堂的居民折扣，这是一个很好的折扣，”他笑着说。 “我必须纠正它们。”

由于护理需要耐心和同情心——这些特质传统上被视为女性特质——所以有人认为所有男护士都是同性恋。

博德隆知道，持续存在的刻板印象会让一些男人犹豫不决。

“很多时候，男性需要获得许可才能进入护理领域，”他说。

他很高兴像《皮特》这样的热门节目描绘了男性扮演各种医疗保健角色，包括护理。 而且，他说，工作报酬丰厚这一事实也没有坏处。 全国注册护士的平均工资每年高达 90,000 美元。

琼斯是家里第一个上大学的人，他说他成为一名护士的原因之一是为了能够照顾自己。

“如果有一天我也想组建一个家庭，我也可以这么做，”他说。

阿拉巴马大学伯明翰分校心血管手术室护士兼兼职讲师史蒂文·帕森斯（Steven Parsons）表示，近年来，护理技术变得更加先进，这是对男性的另一个吸引力。

他说，机器人经常用于肺部手术，有时也用于心脏手术。 他所在的一个机器人团队以男性为主。

“有些人对机械东西有点感兴趣，”他说。

不过，对于其他人来说，进入护理行业的动机是个人的。 前大学棒球运动员康纳·阿德霍特 (Connor Aderholt) 表示，在看到祖母中风后得到的富有同情心的护理后，他和弟弟都决定从事护理职业。

“在那个脆弱的时刻——在我们家庭的脆弱时刻——它让我意识到，这就是我想做的事情，来应对人们必须应对的这些艰难处境，”阿德霍特说。 “为了其他人，在暴风雨中保持冷静。”

更快地移动针

今年秋天，博德隆将担任全国倡导组织美国男性护理协会的领导职务。 他知道还有很多事情需要解决。

“我们会达到 100% 性别中立吗？ 我不知道，”博德隆说。 “我们能帮助进展得更快一点吗？ 当然，这是有机会的。”

他和普渡大学护理学院的一位同事最近取得了重大胜利：将男性健康正式指定为护理专业。 博德隆认为，除了泌尿系统问题之外，还有许多其他因素导致男性预期寿命缩短。

“我们有女性保健执业护士。 我们可能会有男性健康护士从业人员，”博德隆说。 “那是我们的梦想。 这就是我们的目标。”

成绩单

张艾莎，主持人：

护理是美国最大的医疗保健行业，而且还在不断增长。 但只有大约八分之一的护士是男性。 尽管如此，由于吸引更多男性的努力，这一数字仍然是几十年前的两倍。 NPR 的 Andrea Hsu 报道了南方一所学校如何处于领先地位。

（水流的声音片段）

ANDREA HSU 连线：阿拉巴马大学伯明翰护理学院内的模拟套件看起来就像一家医院。 在模拟手术室外的水槽里，兼职教练史蒂文·帕森斯向学生们展示如何进行所谓的长时间擦洗，在进入心脏直视手术之前彻底清洗双手和手臂。

史蒂文·帕森斯：因为我想看到你的手臂举得更多。 上，上，上，上，上，上，上，上。 我们开始吧。

HSU：过了一会儿，全班练习用滚轮将病人从担架上抬到床上。

帕森斯：好的。

身份不明的学生#1：我……

帕森斯：你要倒数三，因为你正在稳定一切。

身份不明的学生#2：好的。

帕森斯：好的。

身份不明的学生#2：一、二、三。

HSU：有十几名学生选修这门手术室技能的选修课。 其中接近一半是男性。 某些专业吸引更多男性。 这是其中之一。 但阿拉巴马大学伯明翰分校的男护理生人数确实多于全国平均水平。 阿米里·萨阿迪克 (Amiri Saadiq) 刚刚与模特进行了对峙，他说，作为这里的学生，他并不感到孤独。

AMIRI SAADIQ：我真的不觉得护理不适合男性或类似的东西。 我的意思是，当我去医院时，我总是看到男人。

HSU：这种文化是大学多年来致力于发展的东西。 这所护士学校的所有营销材料中都以男学生为特色。 他们派男女护士到高中招收学生。 近五分之一的教职人员是男性。 Curry Bordelon 教授表示，一切都会产生影响。

CURRY BORDELON：学生感到被接受。 他们感觉自己属于这里。

HSU：三十年前，回到路易斯安那州的家乡，博德隆经历了一些不同的事情。 他第一次学习护理是通过他的护士阿姨。

博德隆：如果你愿意的话，我是她的实习病人。

HSU：高中毕业后，他成为一名志愿消防员。

BORDELON：我真的很喜欢接听医疗电话。

HSU：从那时起，他决定在一所社区大学参加护理课程。 他记得第一天环顾四周。

BORDELON：你参加的第一堂护理课，也就是你参加的第一堂护理课，大约有 90 人。 男学生只有三个。

HSU：他记得教练告诉队员们，他们来这里的唯一原因是为了完成配额。

BORDELON：对我来说，这算是一种警醒。 这是一个，我想找到适合我的地方，我可以努力奋斗。

HSU：博德隆找到了一所不同的护士学校，并找到了自己的使命，在新生儿重症监护室工作，那里的男护士极其罕见。 他说，新生儿重症监护室的父亲们有时会因为看到他与妻子谈论母乳喂养而产生戒心。 但他特意告诉他们……

BORDELON：我来这里是为了支持整个家庭，这意味着，爸爸，你愿意进行肌肤接触吗？

HSU：他说这对于宝宝的长期成功非常重要。

BORDELON：所以我从新生儿重症监护室的父亲们那里得到了很多积极的反馈，他们支持包容性。 更不用说，这让他们为过渡后的家做好更多准备。

HSU：博德隆说，各地拥有更多的男护士可以改善结果。 研究发现，患者对与自己相似的医护人员反应更好。 护理专业学生尼古拉斯·西伊（Nicholas Seay）在临床轮转期间已经经历过这种情况。 作为一名年轻的黑人，他发现当他进入房间时，年长的黑人有时会显得如释重负，并向他敞开心扉。

尼古拉斯·西：我在重症监护室里收治了一位病人，大多数时候，他们病得很厉害。

HSU：不能说话，但这个人不行。

SEAY：我发现自己必须要，好吧。 我必须，就像——我必须离开，因为我们只是在这里大喊大叫。 我已经给你开药了，先生。 就像，我会回来的。

HSU：现在，Seay 也遇到过一些女性患者对他在床边感到不舒服的情况。 在这种情况下，他会退到一边。

SEAY：如果我是一名女性，我也不知道我是否会想要一个男学生在房间里。 所以我真的不能生气。

HSU：即使在大学环境中，刻板印象也仍然存在。 男护士经常被误认为是医生。 还有人认为所有男护士都是同性恋。 库里·博德隆知道这种刻板印象会让一些男人犹豫不决。

BORDELON：很多时候，男性需要获得许可才能进入护理领域。

HSU：他很高兴像《皮特》这样的热门节目描绘了男性扮演各种医疗保健角色，包括护理。 他还表示，工作报酬丰厚这一事实并没有什么坏处。

BORDELON：我们会实现 100% 性别中立吗？ 我不知道。 我们能帮助进展得更快一点吗？ 当然。 有这样的机会。

HSU：博德隆和普渡大学的一位同事最近取得了重大胜利，将男性健康正式指定为护理专业。 他已经在想象这将会带来多大的改变。

Andrea Hsu，NPR 新闻，阿拉巴马州伯明翰。
